originalTitle: Valve just imported 13 tons of VR headsets in one day
summary: On June 10th, the German container ship Posen docked in Los Angeles after a two-week voyage from Shanghai. As Valve watcher Brad Lynch notes, it was almost certainly carrying the first mass production shipments of the Steam Frame, Valve's new gaming headset. Import records show that Valve's distribution partner Ceva offloaded nearly 32 metric tons […]
content: On June 10th, the German container ship Posen docked in Los Angeles after a two-week voyage from Shanghai. As Valve watcher Brad Lynch notes, it was almost certainly carrying the first mass production shipments of the Steam Frame, Valve’s new gaming headset. Import records show that Valve’s distribution partner Ceva offloaded nearly 32 metric tons of “Virtual Reality Devices” on Valve’s behalf — or roughly 13 tons of actual product, after you subtract the roughly 3,700 kilogram weight of five 40-foot shipping containers. [Image: A pause in Steam Machine and Steam Deck shipments, a flurry of VR instead. https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/image-2.png?quality=90&strip=all] That’s the same math we used to estimate that Valve imported 50 tons of game consoles in two days last month — and since Valve is differentiating between “Game Consoles” and “Virtual Reality Devices” in its import records, we can be far more certain that the Steam Machine console is the device it was stockpiling before. Speaking of the Steam Machine, Valve’s stockpile may now have grown to 141 metric tons, as that’s roughly how much “Game Consoles” product has arrived in 12,600kg containers since April 23rd. And it looks like Valve probably received three shipments of Steam Deck handhelds in May, two on May 18th and one on May 30th, judging by how those containers had the higher gross weight of 14,500kg. That’s generally how heavy Valve’s “Game Console” containers were before the Steam Machine was announced. [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/image-1.png?quality=90&strip=all] [Image: https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/image_2ad1c7.png?quality=90&strip=all] 13 tons isn’t actually a lot of VR headsets, of course, but perhaps more of them fit into a container than the Steam Machine console. They each weigh 654g (roughly 1.44lb) with a pair of wand controllers; back-of-the-napkin math suggests we’re probably talking about fewer than 20,000 units right now. There might not be that many Steam Machines in the US yet, either: 141 metric tons could easily be fewer than 50,000 units at their higher 2.6kg weight per console, not counting any controllers or cables. Valve confirmed days ago that both the Steam Machine and Steam Frame will launch this summer, and has signaled that it had to rethink prices because of RAMageddon. Even if they’re pricey, though, they may sell out quickly.
chineseTitle: Valve一天之内进口了13吨VR耳机
chineseSummary: 6月10日，德国集装箱船“波森”号从上海出发，结束为期两周的航行，抵达洛杉矶。正如 Valve 观察家 Brad Lynch 指出的那样，几乎可以肯定，它是 Valve 新款游戏耳机 Steam Frame 的首批批量生产产品。进口记录显示 Valve 的分销合作伙伴 Ceva 卸载了近 32 公吨 […]
chineseContent: 6月10日，德国集装箱船“波森”号从上海出发，结束为期两周的航行，抵达洛杉矶。正如 Valve 观察家 Brad Lynch 指出的那样，几乎可以肯定，它携带的是 Valve 新款游戏耳机 Steam Frame 的首批量产出货。进口记录显示，Valve 的分销合作伙伴 Ceva 代表 Valve 卸载了近 32 吨“虚拟现实设备”，扣除 5 个 40 英尺集装箱的约 3,700 公斤重量后，实际产品约 13 吨。 [图片：Steam Machine 和 Steam Deck 的发货暂停，取而代之的是 VR 的热潮。 https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/image-2.png?quality=90&strip=all] 这与我们用来估计 Valve 上个月两天内进口了 50 吨游戏机的数学方法相同 - 而且由于 Valve 在其进口记录中区分了“游戏机”和“虚拟现实设备”，因此我们可以更加确定 Steam Machine 控制台就是该设备之前就在囤货。说到 Steam Machine，Valve 的库存现在可能已增至 141 吨，因为这大约是自 4 月 23 日以来以 12,600 公斤集装箱抵达的“游戏机”产品的数量。从这些集装箱的总重高达 14,500 公斤来看，Valve 可能在 5 月份收到了三批 Steam Deck 手持设备，其中两批在 5 月 18 日，另一批在 5 月 30 日。这就是 Steam Machine 发布之前 Valve 的“游戏机”容器的重量。 [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/image-1.png?quality=90&strip=all] [图片：https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/image_2ad1c7.png?quality=90&strip=all] 13吨实际上并不是很多当然是 VR 耳机
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
